title: Battery Chemistry Design Review — 2023-10-05
text:
# Battery Chemistry Design Review — 2023-10-05

**Space:** K200  
**Meeting type:** Technical Design Review  
**Attendees:** Dr. Rhea Voss (CSE), Lena Okafor (Power), Felix Herrera (Integration), Aisha Mbeki (Test), Rowan Ellery (Electrical), Nina Schulz (Quality)  
**Chair:** Dr. Rhea Voss  
**Minutes recorded by:** Nina Schulz

---

## Background

This review was convened following the LiPo thermal runaway event documented in KES-208 (battery cell assembly P-1062, during the 8 September 2023 hot-day endurance test). The purpose is to decide whether to continue with LiPo chemistry or transition to an alternative, and to agree the path forward.

## Presentation Summary

Lena Okafor presented a trade study comparing the current LiPo (Nominal: 22.2V 20Ah 6S2P) against LiFePO4 and LTO chemistries on four criteria: energy density, thermal stability, cycle life, and IEC 62133-2 compliance burden.

| Chemistry | Energy density | Thermal stability | Cycle life | IEC 62133-2 risk |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| LiPo (current) | High (≈200 Wh/kg) | Poor (thermal runaway at ~150°C) | 300–500 cycles | High |
| LiFePO4 | Medium (≈140 Wh/kg) | Excellent (stable to 270°C) | 1000–2000 cycles | Low |
| LTO | Low (≈80 Wh/kg) | Excellent | >5000 cycles | Very low |

## Discussion

Dr. Voss noted that the KES-208 event was the second thermal anomaly on P-1062 cells (the first was a minor swelling event in March 2023 captured in the lab log but not escalated to a Jira issue). She stated that the programme cannot continue with LiPo chemistry given the IEC 62133-2 compliance requirements for the customer delivery — the customer's own safety case explicitly prohibits LiPo cells for BVLOS maritime operations due to salt-water submersion risk.

Felix Herrera confirmed that the ~12% reduction in energy density from switching to LiFePO4 would reduce endurance from a projected 75 minutes to approximately 66 minutes at cruise — within the 60-minute minimum requirement.

Aisha Mbeki asked about the re-qualification test burden. Lena Okafor confirmed ECR-214 will capture the full impact including new qualification testing per DOC-421 Rev C.

LTO was ruled out: the energy density is too low to meet the 60-minute endurance requirement without exceeding the 8kg MTOW.

## Decision

**AGREED:** Transition the K-200 battery cell assembly (P-1062) from LiPo to LiFePO4 chemistry.

- Decision maker: Dr. Rhea Voss (Chief Systems Engineer)
- Rationale: IEC 62133-2 compliance requirement, two observed thermal events on P-1062 (KES-208 and March 2023 lab anomaly), acceptable energy density penalty
- ECR to be raised: ECR-214 (Lena Okafor to raise by 2023-10-12)
- Updated specification: DOC-421 (Battery System Specification) to be updated to Rev C
- BMS impact: P-1075 charge/discharge profiles must be updated for LiFePO4 voltage curves

## Actions

| # | Action | Owner | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Raise ECR-214 for battery chemistry change | Lena Okafor | 2023-10-12 |
| A2 | Update DOC-421 to Rev C covering LiFePO4 spec | Lena Okafor | 2023-11-15 |
| A3 | Update P-1075 BMS charge profiles for LiFePO4 | Rowan Ellery | 2023-11-30 |
| A4 | Plan cell-level qualification test campaign | Aisha Mbeki | 2023-11-01 |
